sections.paragraph:
φιλοπονέω (X., Pla.+; pap; Sir prol. ln. 20) to expend considerable effort, exert oneself, devote oneself περί τι in (to) someth. (Isocr. 1, 46 τὸ περὶ τὴν ἀρετὴν φιλοπονεῖν; PLond I, 130, 5 p. 133 [I/II a.d.]) 2 Cl 19:1. The noun φιλοπονία appears in an ins fr. Beroia SEG XXVII, 261 B, 47 and 56.—DELG s.v. πένομαι.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

prol. prol. = prologus (prolog, prologue)

  1. ln. = line

someth. someth. = something

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. fr. = from

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1058
